title: trifolium arvense var. littorale
intro: Trifolium arvense var. littorale, also known as Littoral Wood Clover, is a variant of the Trifolium arvense plant, a species of clover native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of isoflavones and other compounds. In herbal preparations, Littoral Wood Clover is often used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in folk remedies and Ayurvedic medicine.
